summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=2 (raw=2, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: pickle.loads is called on data derived from user-supplied base64 input. The code does not implement any safe unpickling (e.g., restricted unpickler, allowlist of safe types). Arbitrary code execution is possible.

raw.summary: The code uses pickle.loads on untrusted base64-decoded metadata without any safe unpickling guard. The SAST findings (bandit:B301 and bandit:B403) are true positives: arbitrary code execution is possible during deserialization. The filename validation is also flawed (ALLOWED_FILENAME_CHARS is empty set due to Path().as_posix() returning '.' for current directory, so any filename will be rejected or the check is broken), but the primary security failure is the unsafe pickle deserialization.

sast_review.reason: pickle.loads is called on data derived from user-supplied base64 input. The code does not implement any safe unpickling (e.g., restricted unpickler, allowlist of safe types). Arbitrary code execution is possible.
The import of pickle itself is flagged because it is used unsafely on untrusted data. This is the same root cause as bandit:B301 (line 15).
